commit.message:
fix(ui): use event filters for undecorated window resize (#55)

* Initial plan

* fix(ui): use event filters for window resize to work at all edges

Switch from scene event handlers (setOnMouseMoved/Pressed/Dragged) to
event filters (addEventFilter) for undecorated window resize handling.

Event handlers fire during the bubbling phase, so child nodes like
TabPane and ScrollPane can consume drag events before they reach the
scene handler. Event filters fire during the capturing phase (before
child nodes), ensuring resize works at all window edges.

Also consume press/drag events when in resize mode to prevent child
node interference.

400030001 and disconnects the WebSocket.  When a track just changed,
duration may still be 0 (stream not yet probed, Ynison state not yet
updated).  Skip sending progress until duration is known.

* fix: guard sharpe ratio against single-observation std dev (ddof=1 with n=1)

Require at least 2 return observations before calling np.std(ddof=1).
With only 1 return value, numpy emits a RuntimeWarning (degrees of
freedom <= 0) and produces NaN. Returns None early instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: clean up model loading and validation logic

- SklearnModel._load(): wrap the entire joblib/pickle load block in a
  single try/except so errors from both loaders are caught and wrapped
  in ModelLoadError (previously pickle.load() errors could escape unwrapped)
- SklearnModel.validate(): remove redundant (ModelLoadError, Exception)
  — Exception already covers ModelLoadError
- model_check.py: replace fragile string-based failure detection
  ('FAILED' in m) with an explicit boolean flag
- _find_predict_calls(): use dict for O(1) deduplication instead of
  O(n) list membership checks

commit.message:
Implement explicit resource management (using/await using declarations)

Add DisposableStack and AsyncDisposableStack built-in support with full
prototype chain, subclassing via new.target, and dispose/use/adopt/defer/move
methods. Implement AsyncIteratorPrototype[Symbol.asyncDispose] with proper
Promise-based semantics.

Compiler changes:
- Emit DisposeResources/DisposeResourcesAsync opcodes for using declarations
- Support async dispose fallback (try Symbol.asyncDispose, fall back to Symbol.dispose)
- Handle for-of using/await using immutability and TDZ
- Fix hoisted function lexical cleanup for using declarations
- Add for-of using redeclaration checks in parser

Parser changes:
- Reject using declarations in switch case clauses
- Check for-of head/body var conflicts with using declarations

Add dependency CVE scanner via OSV.dev

New 'deps' command that scans NuGet and npm package manifests for
known vulnerabilities using the OSV.dev API.

Features:
- Manifest discovery: *.csproj, *.fsproj, *.vbproj, packages.lock.json,
  package-lock.json (v1/v2/v3), package.json
- Central package management: Directory.Packages.props with VersionOverride
- 2-step OSV lookup: querybatch for IDs, then /v1/vulns/{id} for details
- Graceful degradation: warns on network errors, doesn't fail the scan
- Reuses existing Finding type, output formatters, and CI integration
- New --ecosystem flag to filter by ecosystem (e.g., nuget,npm)

Modules:
- src/Deps/ManifestParser.fs — discovers and parses package manifests
- src/Deps/OsvClient.fs — OSV.dev HTTP client with batch queries
- src/Deps/DepScanner.fs — orchestrates parsing → querying → normalization

Tested against ZTS: 402 deps across 34 manifests, 7 real CVEs found
with severity levels and fix version suggestions.

commit.message:
fix: handle async FSWatcher errors in project customization watcher

On Linux, fs.watch() on a non-existent directory emits an asynchronous
'error' event instead of throwing synchronously. Without an error handler,
this crashes the process. Tests that create AryxAppService with fake
project paths (e.g. C:\workspace\project-one) trigger this on Linux CI
because those paths don't exist.

Attach an 'error' event handler to the FSWatcher that logs a warning and
closes the watcher gracefully. This fixes the Linux CI 'bun test' failure
and also prevents production crashes if a watched directory is deleted
at runtime.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(web): dialog stays visible after closing due to Tailwind flex override

Tailwind's `flex` class overrides the browser's native
`dialog:not([open]) { display: none }` rule, causing the dialog
to remain visible after dialog.close() is called.

Replace `flex` with `hidden open:flex` so the dialog is hidden
when closed and uses flexbox layout only when open.

fix(sidebar): use counts and minimal-list APIs DEV-1772 (#6918)

### 📣 Summary

Use new API for Sidebar lists of projects (Deployed, Draft, and
Archived). This fixes a problem when user with more than 200 projects
was unable to see all of their projects in the Sidebar (previously it
was limited to 200 latest projects, prioritizing Deployed ones). Now
three groups use infinite scroll loading. We also fixed a problem with
sidebar serving always "My Projects" projects - now when user switches
to organization projects or a custom view, the sidebar reflects this
properly.

### 💭 Notes

Changes here:
- `AssetName` and `getAssetDisplayName` now works with
`AssetMinimalList`
- `BulkDeletePrompt` invalidates all sidebar related queries now
- `SidebarFormsList` has multiple changes:
- It now operates on context (`my-projects`, `my-org-projects`, or
`custom-view-projects`) which is resolved from current path
  - Has a utility function for invalidating all queries in sidebar
- Has a custom `useQuery` (instead of using orval hooks) that operates
on different function based on context
  - Reflux actions are now invalidating with utility function
- It now only fetches counts from new APIs (different counts API for
different context) and passes those to `SidebarFormsListCategory`
- `SidebarFormsListCategory` has multiple changes too:
  - It operates now on context and deployment status
- Instead of getting list of assets from parent, it fetches it now using
API
- Has a custom `useInfiniteQuery` (instead of using orval hooks) that
operates on different function based on context and is triggered by
`InfiniteScrollTrigger`
- The list of projects now always lead to `ROUTES.FORM`, as minimal list
endpoint doesn't serve permissions

### 👀 Preview steps

1. ℹ️ Have an account with a lot of projects, ideally over 200 (use
kobo-fixture, or drag and drop a lot of small projects [many
small.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26484188/many.small.zip)
or some other idea)
2. [Enable
MMO](https://www.notion.so/kobotoolbox/Setting-up-MMOs-for-local-development-1357e515f6548093b631f1686b60e49d)
3. Create new user, add it to MMO, create few projects with the new
user, go back to original user afterwards
4. [Enable a custom
view](https://www.notion.so/kobotoolbox/Regional-Projects-User-Documentation-Setting-up-Custom-Project-Views-f45b8a2bd09d45a4ade89f4b4430df1d)
with only part of projects being included (see "South Asia" example in
the link)
5. Change few projects to have the "Country" field matching the custom
view
6. Go to My Projects
7. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab,
`api/v2/assets/?q=asset_type:survey&limit=200` is no longer being called
8. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab `/api/v2/assets/counts/` is being called
9. Open "Deployed" category
10. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab `/api/v2/assets/minimal-list/` is being
called and a list of projects appear
11. Scroll down
12. 🟢 Notice that another API call is being made and next 20 projects
are loaded (infinite scroll)
13. Select one project in the table (with checkbox)
14. Choose "Delete" button in top right and confirm modal
15. 🟢 Notice that both the count ("Deployed" + "(n)") and the list of
projects refreshed and deleted project no longer appears
16. Select two projects in the table (also with checkbox; bulk deleting
uses different code)
17. Choose "Delete" button in top right and confirm modal
18. 🟢 Notice that both the count ("Deployed" + "(n)") and the list of
projects refreshed and deleted project no longer appears
19. Select "My projects" dropdown and choose "<username>'s organization
projects"
20. 🟢 Notice sidebar refreshed and counts are slightly different (now it
includes projects from step (3))
21. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab,
`api/v2/organizations/<uid>/assets/counts/` is being called
22. Open "Deployed" category
23. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab
`api/v2/organizations/<uid>/assets/minimal-list/` is being called and a
list of projects appear
24. Proceed with "Delete" button steps 13-18 described above
25. Select "My projects" dropdown and choose "South Asia" custom view
26. 🟢 Notice sidebar refreshed and counts are slightly different
27. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab,
`api/v2/project-views/<uid>/assets/counts/` is being called
28. Open "Deployed" category
29. 🟢 Notice that in Network tab
`api/v2/project-views/<uid>/assets/minimal-list/` is being called and a
list of projects appear
30. Proceed with "Delete" button steps 13-18 described above

commit.message:
perf: improve application startup performance

Renderer code splitting:
- Lazy-load 15 components with React.lazy() and Suspense (ChatPane,
  ActivityPanel, SettingsPanel, TerminalPanel, WelcomePane, BottomPanel,
  GitPanel, CommandPalette, CommitComposer, WorkflowPicker, BookmarksPanel,
  SessionSearchPanel, KeyboardShortcutsPanel, DiscoveredToolingModal,
  ProjectSettingsPanel)
- This moves ~1.2 MB of optional dependencies (Lexical, @xyflow/react,
  @xterm/xterm, highlight.js, motion) out of the critical bundle
- Critical bundle reduced from ~1.5 MB to ~759 KB
- Defer JetBrains Mono font load to TerminalPanel (saves ~80 KB at startup)

Main process optimizations:
- Use show: false + ready-to-show on BrowserWindow to eliminate blank flash
- Decouple workspace loading from bootstrap — no longer blocks window, tray,
  and auto-update setup
- Defer sidecar-dependent approval tool pruning to run in background after
  workspace is returned to renderer (removes sidecar spawn from critical path)
- Parallelize independent workspace sync operations (user tooling, project
  tooling, project customization) with Promise.all()

- Pagefind searchable block
